title: Display extra fields in formatWorkItem output
description:
## Context
Phase 5: User Story 3 (P3) — Configure Additional Fields for Get-Item. Update the output formatter to display additional fields alongside the default fields.

Spec Reference: US3 acceptance scenario 1 and FR-005.

## File Paths
- `src/commands/get-item.ts` (MODIFY — `formatWorkItem` function)

## Implementation Details

### Modify `formatWorkItem(workItem: WorkItem, short: boolean): string`
After the URL line (line ~50) and before the description section, add extra fields display:

```typescript
// Display extra fields (after URL, before description)
if (workItem.extraFields) {
  for (const [refName, value] of Object.entries(workItem.extraFields)) {
    // Extract display label: last segment after the last '.'
    // e.g., "System.Tags" -> "Tags", "Microsoft.VSTS.Common.Priority" -> "Priority"
    const label = refName.includes('.') ? refName.split('.').pop()! : refName;
    lines.push(`${label.padEnd(13)}${value}`);
  }
}
```

### Display Label Extraction
- `System.Tags` → `Tags`
- `Microsoft.VSTS.Common.Priority` → `Priority`
- `Microsoft.VSTS.Scheduling.StoryPoints` → `StoryPoints`
- `Custom.MyField` → `MyField`

### Short Mode
In `--short` mode, still show extra fields. They are user-configured and therefore always desired (user explicitly chose to see them).

### Contract Reference (contracts/cli-contract.md)
Human-readable output format:
```
ID:          12345
Type:        User Story
Title:       My work item
State:       Active
Assigned To: John Doe
Area:        MyProject\Team1
Iteration:   MyProject\Sprint 1
URL:         https://dev.azure.com/...
Tags:        frontend, bug
Priority:    2

Description:
...
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Extra fields displayed after URL and before description
- [ ] Field labels extracted from reference name (last segment after last `.`)
- [ ] Labels padded to 13 characters (matching existing format)
- [ ] Extra fields shown in both normal and `--short` mode
- [ ] No extra fields section when `workItem.extraFields` is null
- [ ] Output matches contract format specification
- [ ] Existing output unchanged when no extra fields (SC-004)

## Technical Notes
- The `formatWorkItem` function is at line ~35 in get-item.ts
- Follow existing `.padEnd(13)` pattern for label alignment
- Extra fields appear between URL and blank line before description
close_reason: Added extra fields display in formatWorkItem

title: Reduce cognitive complexity of set-md-field action handler
description:
SonarCloud rule **S3776** (CRITICAL): The action handler function in `src/commands/set-md-field.ts:26` has a Cognitive Complexity of 28, exceeding the allowed maximum of 15.

The function (~110 lines) contains deeply nested logic for:
1. ID validation (lines 27-33)
2. Mutual exclusion check for inline content vs --file (lines 35-40)
3. Content resolution with 4 branches: --file, inline, stdin, error (lines 42-74)
4. Org/project validation (lines 76-83)
5. API call and output formatting (lines 87-109)
6. Error handling with 6 if/else-if branches (lines 110-138)

**Recommended approach**: Extract helper functions:
- `resolveContent(inlineContent, options)` — handles file/inline/stdin content resolution
- `formatOutput(result, options, field)` — handles JSON vs human-readable output
- `handleError(err, context, id)` — handles error message mapping using a Map or Record instead of if/else chain
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Cognitive Complexity of the action handler is reduced to ≤15
- [ ] All existing tests continue to pass
- [ ] No behavior changes
close_reason: Extracted 6 helper functions to reduce cognitive complexity from 28 to ~10

title: Implement credential store wrapper
description:
Create a wrapper around @napi-rs/keyring for storing and retrieving PATs from the Windows Credential Manager.

API:
- getPat(): Promise<string | null> — retrieve stored PAT (service: 'azdo-cli', account: 'pat')
- storePat(pat: string): Promise<void> — persist PAT to credential manager

Must handle errors gracefully: if credential manager is unavailable (e.g., running on Linux without secret-service), return null from getPat and silently skip storePat rather than crashing. This allows the auth chain to fall through to the interactive prompt.

- [ ] getPat returns null when no credential stored

- [ ] storePat persists PAT to credential manager
- [ ] Graceful error handling if credential manager unavailable (returns null / no-op)

title: Implement config store service
description:
## Context
Phase 2: Foundational. Core config persistence layer that MUST be complete before ANY user story can start. This service handles reading/writing the `~/.azdo/config.json` file using only Node.js built-in modules (zero new runtime dependencies per constitution principle IV).

## File Paths
- `src/services/config-store.ts` (CREATE — new file)

## Implementation Details
Must export the following functions:

### `getConfigPath(): string`
Returns `path.join(os.homedir(), '.azdo', 'config.json')` using `node:os` and `node:path`.

### `loadConfig(): CliConfig`
- Reads `~/.azdo/config.json` using `fs.readFileSync(path, 'utf-8')`
- Parses with `JSON.parse()`
- Returns empty `{}` on missing file (`ENOENT`)
- On corrupt/invalid JSON: warns to stderr, returns empty `{}`
- Uses synchronous I/O per research.md R3 (config files are tiny, sync is simpler)

### `saveConfig(config: CliConfig): void`
- Creates directory with `fs.mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true })`
- Writes with `fs.writeFileSync(path, JSON.stringify(data, null, 2) + '\n')`

### `getConfigValue(key: string): string | string[] | undefined`
- Validates key against allowlist `['org', 'project', 'fields']`
- Throws on unknown keys with message: `Unknown setting key "${key}". Valid keys: org, project, fields`
- Returns the value from loaded config

### `setConfigValue(key: string, value: string): void`
- Validates key against allowlist
- For `fields` key: splits comma-separated string into array
- For `org`/`project`: stores as string
- Empty value treated as unset (removes the key)
- Loads existing config, updates, saves

### `unsetConfigValue(key: string): void`
- Validates key against allowlist
- Loads existing config, deletes key, saves

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `config-store.ts` created in `src/services/` with all 6 exported functions
- [ ] All functions have explicit TypeScript type annotations
- [ ] `loadConfig()` returns empty `CliConfig` when file doesn't exist
- [ ] `loadConfig()` warns to stderr and returns empty on corrupt JSON (FR-009)
- [ ] `setConfigValue` splits `fields` value by comma into string array
- [ ] `setConfigValue` treats empty string value as unset
- [ ] Unknown keys throw with descriptive error message (FR-010)
- [ ] Valid keys: `org`, `project`, `fields` (FR-003)
- [ ] Directory created recursively if it doesn't exist
- [ ] JSON output is human-readable (indented with 2 spaces)
- [ ] Zero new runtime dependencies — only `node:fs`, `node:path`, `node:os`
- [ ] TypeScript compiles with `strict: true` and no errors

## Technical Notes
- Parallels existing `src/services/credential-store.ts` pattern (service module for persistence)
- Research decision R1: `~/.azdo/config.json` location
- Research decision R2: JSON format (built-in, zero deps)
- Research decision R3: Synchronous `node:fs` I/O
- Research decision R6: Fixed allowlist validation
- Constitution Principle III: Single Responsibility — config-store handles only persistence
- Constitution Principle V: Simplicity — sync I/O, no schema library, flat module

description:
SonarCloud rule **S5843** (MAJOR): The regex in `src/services/html-detect.ts:1` has a complexity of 22, exceeding the allowed 20. Current regex:
```
/<\/?(p|br|div|span|strong|em|b|i|u|a|ul|ol|li|h[1-6]|table|tr|td|th|img|pre|code)\b/i
```

Options to fix:
1. **Split into two regexes**: Separate block-level and inline tags, OR the results
2. **Set-based approach**: Use a simple tag-matching regex + a Set of known HTML tags
3. **Remove rare tags**: Drop `th`, `u` etc. to get under the threshold
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Regex complexity is reduced to ≤20

- [ ] HTML detection behavior is unchanged

title: Extend PAT resolution to full chain
description:
Extend resolvePat() in auth.ts to implement the complete PAT resolution chain per FR-013:

1. Check AZDO_PAT environment variable → return AuthCredential(source: 'env') if set
2. Check credential store via getPat() → return AuthCredential(source: 'credential-store') if found
3. Prompt interactively via promptForPat() → if user provides PAT, persist via storePat(), return AuthCredential(source: 'prompt')
4. If all three fail → throw error with message: 'Error: Authentication cancelled. Set AZDO_PAT environment variable or run again to enter a PAT.'

This replaces the simple env-var-only version from T005 with the full chain.

- [ ] Falls back to credential store (getPat) if env var not set
- [ ] Falls back to interactive prompt if credential store empty
- [ ] Persists PAT to credential store after successful prompt
- [ ] Returns AuthCredential with correct source for each path
- [ ] Throws error with actionable message if all sources fail

title: Add extra fields parameter to getWorkItem API
description:
## Context
Phase 5: User Story 3 (P3) — Configure Additional Fields for Get-Item. Modify the Azure DevOps API client to accept and request additional work item fields.

Spec Reference: US3 acceptance scenarios 1, 2 and FR-005, FR-007.

## File Paths
- `src/services/azdo-client.ts` (MODIFY)

## Implementation Details
Modify the `getWorkItem` function signature and behavior:

### Current Signature
```typescript
export async function getWorkItem(context: AzdoContext, id: number, pat: string): Promise<WorkItem>
```

### New Signature
```typescript
export async function getWorkItem(context: AzdoContext, id: number, pat: string, extraFields?: string[]): Promise<WorkItem>
```

### API URL Change
When `extraFields` is provided, append a `fields` query parameter to the API URL containing both the default fields and the extra fields:
```
?api-version=7.1&fields=System.Title,System.State,...,System.Tags,Microsoft.VSTS.Common.Priority
```

Default fields to always include:
- `System.Title`, `System.State`, `System.WorkItemType`, `System.AssignedTo`
- `System.Description`, `Microsoft.VSTS.Common.AcceptanceCriteria`, `Microsoft.VSTS.TCM.ReproSteps`
- `System.AreaPath`, `System.IterationPath`

### Response Handling
- The `AzdoWorkItemResponse.fields` interface needs to accept additional arbitrary fields
- Use index signature or cast for extra fields: `data.fields[fieldName]`
- For each extra field in the request:
  - If present in response: add to `WorkItem.extraFields` as `Record<string, string>`
  - If not present: silently omit (FR-007)
  - Convert non-string values via `String()` for consistent display

### Research Reference
Research decision R4: Use `fields` query parameter (most efficient, avoids `$expand=all`)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `getWorkItem` accepts optional `extraFields?: string[]` parameter
- [ ] When `extraFields` provided: API URL includes `fields` query parameter with all field names
- [ ] When `extraFields` undefined: existing behavior unchanged (no `fields` param)
- [ ] Extra field values populated in `WorkItem.extraFields` as `Record<string, string>`
- [ ] Missing fields silently omitted from `extraFields` (FR-007)
- [ ] Non-string field values converted via `String()`
- [ ] `WorkItem.extraFields` is `null` when no extra fields requested
- [ ] All existing tests pass without modification (SC-004)

## Technical Notes
- The Azure DevOps REST API `GET _apis/wit/workitems/{id}?fields=...` accepts comma-separated field names
- Currently the API URL has no `fields` param, so it returns default fields
- When `fields` param is added, ONLY those fields are returned — must include defaults
- The `AzdoWorkItemResponse` interface is internal (not exported) so can be extended safely
close_reason: Added extraFields parameter to getWorkItem API

title: Add --fields option to get-item command
description:
## Context
Phase 5: User Story 3 (P3) — Configure Additional Fields for Get-Item. Add the `--fields` CLI option to `get-item` and integrate with saved config fields.

Spec Reference: US3 acceptance scenarios 3, 4 and FR-006, FR-011.

## File Paths
- `src/commands/get-item.ts` (MODIFY)

## Implementation Details

### New Option
Add to the command definition:
```typescript
.option('--fields <fields>', 'comma-separated additional field reference names')
```

### Field Resolution
In the action handler, resolve fields from two sources:
1. `--fields` flag (highest priority, overrides saved config)
2. `loadConfig().fields` (from saved settings)

```typescript
const fieldsList: string[] | undefined =
  options.fields
    ? options.fields.split(',').map((f: string) => f.trim())
    : loadConfig().fields;
```

### Pass to API
Pass resolved fields to `getWorkItem()`:
```typescript
const workItem = await getWorkItem(context, id, credential.pat, fieldsList);
```

### Contract Reference (contracts/cli-contract.md)
- `--fields "System.Tags,Custom.Field"` overrides saved fields for that invocation
- When no `--fields` flag and no saved fields: `undefined` passed (existing behavior)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `--fields` option added to `get-item` command
- [ ] `--fields "System.Tags,Microsoft.VSTS.Common.Priority"` requests those fields from API
- [ ] When `--fields` not provided but config has `fields`: uses config fields
- [ ] When `--fields` provided: overrides config fields (FR-006)
- [ ] When neither `--fields` nor config fields: no extra fields (existing behavior, SC-004)
- [ ] Fields are comma-separated and trimmed
- [ ] Resolved fields passed to `getWorkItem()` API call

## Technical Notes

- FR-011: comma-separated list format

description:
Implement a fetch()-based REST client for the Azure DevOps Work Items API v7.1. This is the core HTTP layer used by all user stories.

Function signature: getWorkItem(context: AzdoContext, id: number, pat: string): Promise<WorkItem>

API details:
- Endpoint: GET https://dev.azure.com/{org}/{project}/_apis/wit/workitems/{id}?api-version=7.1
- Auth: Basic header with Base64 of ':{pat}' (empty username, PAT as password)
- Response: JSON with id, rev, fields object, _links. Map fields.System.* to WorkItem interface.
- Required PAT scope: vso.work (Work Items read)

Must throw typed/descriptive errors for:
- 401 Unauthorized (invalid/expired PAT)
- 403 Forbidden (insufficient permissions)
- 404 Not Found (work item doesn't exist)
- Network failures (fetch rejects)

- [ ] 403 response throws permission error

- [ ] Network failure throws connection error


### `createConfigCommand(): Command`
- Creates a `config` command group with description "Manage CLI settings"
- Adds the `set` subcommand

### `set` subcommand
- Arguments: `<key>` (required), `<value>` (required)
- Option: `--json` (output in JSON format)
- Validates key against allowlist via `setConfigValue()`
- On success: outputs `Set "org" to "myorganization"` (human-readable) or `{ "key": "org", "value": "myorganization" }` (JSON)
- On error (unknown key): outputs error to stderr, exit code 1
- Uses `process.stdout.write()` for output, `process.stderr.write()` for errors (POSIX convention)

### Contract Reference (contracts/cli-contract.md)
**stdout** (human-readable): `Set "org" to "myorganization"`
**stdout** (`--json`): `{ "key": "org", "value": "myorganization" }`
**stderr** (error): `Error: Unknown setting key "foo". Valid keys: org, project, fields`
**Exit codes**: 0 success, 1 error

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `src/commands/config.ts` created with `createConfigCommand()` export
- [ ] `config set <key> <value>` persists value to `~/.azdo/config.json`
- [ ] `config set org myorg` outputs confirmation message (FR-008)
- [ ] `config set fields "System.Tags,Microsoft.VSTS.Common.Priority"` stores as array
- [ ] `--json` flag outputs JSON format
- [ ] Unknown key produces error on stderr and exit code 1 (FR-010)
- [ ] Empty value unsets the key
- [ ] Follows commander.js patterns from existing commands
- [ ] Constitution Principle I: meaningful exit codes (0/1)

## Technical Notes
- Follow the exact pattern of `src/commands/get-item.ts` for command structure
- Import `setConfigValue` from `../services/config-store.js`
- Constitution Principle I: CLI-First Design — `--json` flag, stdout/stderr separation
- Constitution Principle III: Single Responsibility — command delegates to config-store service
close_reason: Created config command with set subcommand

title: Implement git remote reader
description:
Implement detectAzdoContext(): AzdoContext that reads the origin remote URL from git and passes it through parseAzdoRemote.

Steps:
1. Run 'git remote get-url origin' via child_process.execSync
2. Parse the URL with parseAzdoRemote()
3. Return AzdoContext on success

Error cases:
- Not in a git repo → throw error: 'Not in a git repository. Provide --org and --project explicitly.' (FR-008)
- Remote is not Azure DevOps → throw error: 'Git remote "origin" is not an Azure DevOps URL. Provide --org and --project explicitly.' (FR-007)
- Origin remote doesn't exist → throw error similar to not-in-repo

title: Add CliConfig and WorkItem.extraFields types
description:
## Context

title: Implement CLI error handling
description:
Add comprehensive error handling to the get-item command. Catch errors from azdo-client and auth services, map them to user-friendly stderr messages per the CLI contract error table.

Error messages must be actionable — tell the user what went wrong AND how to fix it.

Error mapping:
- Invalid ID → 'Error: Work item ID must be a positive integer. Got: "{input}"'
- Missing org/project pair → 'Error: --org and --project must both be provided, or both omitted.'
- Auth failure (401) → 'Error: Authentication failed. Check that your PAT is valid and has the "Work Items (read)" scope.'
- Not found (404) → 'Error: Work item {id} not found in {org}/{project}.'
- Permission denied (403) → 'Error: Access denied. Your PAT may lack permissions for project "{project}".'
- Network error → 'Error: Could not connect to Azure DevOps. Check your network connection.'
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] All error scenarios from CLI contract handled
- [ ] Errors written to stderr (not stdout)
- [ ] Exit code 1 on any error
- [ ] Error messages are actionable (include fix instructions)
- [ ] No stack traces shown to user (catch and format)
- [ ] Error messages match CLI contract exactly
notes: File: src/commands/get-item.ts | FR-014 | Ref: specs/002-get-item-command/contracts/cli-contract.md (Error Output section)
